we-should-ask.description:
since we have no attainment in Ein Sof, how then do
we know Him by name? After all, each name designates the
attainment that we attain in Him, according to the definition of that
name.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-a-great-virtue-in-that.concept: There is a great virtue in that
there-is-a-great-virtue-in-that.description:
on the one hand, it is complete
bestowal, because the desire is only to bestow contentment upon the
Maker and not at all for one’s “self.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

these-words-require-explanation.description:
How was there a reality that
the simple light had filled before the worlds were emanated? Also, the
matter of the ascent of the desire in order to be restricted so as to bring to
light the perfection of His deeds.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

unified.description:
When He unites to act with one force, He is
called “Unified.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

interpretation.description:
“Unites to act with one force” means that His
operations differ from each other and He seems to be doing good and
bad.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

his-different\noperations-have-a-single-.concept:
His different
operations have a single outcome
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

two-discernments-in-bestowal.description:
before it is received and after it is
received.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

explanation-of-the-words-of-our-sages.concept: Explanation of the words of our sages
explanation-of-the-words-of-our-sages.description:
“Hence, work and labor have
been prepared for the reward of the souls, for ‘One who eats that
which is not one’s own is afraid to look upon one’s face.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

first\nbewilderment.description:
Our primary aim and prayer is “Spare us the treasure
of a free gift.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

their-answer-is-even-more-perplexing.concept: Their answer is even more perplexing
their-answer-is-even-more-perplexing.description:
They said that there is a great
flaw in free gifts, namely the shame that encounters every receiver of
a free gift.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

know-this.description: It is known that the nature of every branch is equal to its root.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-the-meaning-of-the-verse.concept: This is the meaning of the verse
this-is-the-meaning-of-the-verse.description:
“The water
conceived and begot darkness” (Midrash Rabbah, Shemot, Chapter
22).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-how-we-come-by-the-ten-sefirot.concept: This is how we come by the ten Sefirot
this-is-how-we-come-by-the-ten-sefirot.description:
Keter is Ein Sof; Hochma is
the light of the expansion from Ein Sof; Bina is the light of Hochma
that intensified in order to increase abundance, by which it grew
coarser.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

name.description:
The tip
of the Yod is Ein Sof, meaning the operating force included the
thought of creation, which is to delight His creations, namely the
vessel of Keter.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

so-it-is-in-spirituality.description:
A certain amount of new forms is
regarded as “spiritual movements” which are intermingled in one
another by way of cause and consequence, and they are called “time”
in spirituality.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

for-this-reason-he-states\nprecisely.concept:
For this reason he states
precisely
for-this-reason-he-states\nprecisely.description: “The water of the upper light of Ein Sof.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-distinguish-two-kinds-of-light-in-eac.concept: We distinguish two kinds of light in each Sefira
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefirot.description:
Hesed,
Gevura, Tifferet, Netzah, Hod, Yesod), and phase four is called
Malchut.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

now-we-might-ask.description:
Bina should have come before Hochma, because
the examination of the future and the desire to know it comes first.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-four-worlds.description: Atzilut, Beria, Yetzira, Assiya, clothe this Adam Kadmon.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefirot-in-yesod.description:
one from the upper light from above downward,
and nine Sefirot of reflected light from below upward, which must
pass through it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-same-manner-applies-in-the-ten-sefir.concept: The same manner applies in the ten Sefirot of Hod
the-same-manner-applies-in-the-ten-sefir.description:
two Sefirot from
above downward, meaning the light of Hod and the light of Yesod that
passes through it, and eight Sefirot from below upward.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-same\napplies-to-the-ten-sefirot-of-.concept:
The same
applies to the ten Sefirot of Netzah
the-same\napplies-to-the-ten-sefirot-of-.description:
three from above downward, and
seven from below upward.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the\nfive-parts-in-the-soul-of-the-lower.concept:
The
five parts in the soul of the lower Adam
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-seven-lower-sefirot.description:
Hesed, Gevura,
Tifferet, Netzah, Hod, Yesod, Malchut in each Sefira and Partzuf are
regarded as the Guf of that Sefira and Partzuf.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

five-parts-in-the-soul-of-the-lower-adam.concept: Five parts in the soul of the lower Adam
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-five-types-of-light-in-the-low.concept: There are five types of light in the lower Adam
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-vessels-in-the-ten-sefirot-are-calle.concept: The vessels in the ten Sefirot are called
the-vessels-in-the-ten-sefirot-are-calle.description:
Keter, Hochma, Bina, Zeir
Anpin, and Malchut, and the lights in them are called: Yechida, Haya,
Neshama, Ruach, Nefesh.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-are.description:
reception
inside the vessel, and reception in the externality of the vessel (see
Part 1, Table of Topics, item 102).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

because-of-that-they-were\ngiven-differe.concept:
Because of that they were
given different names
because-of-that-they-were\ngiven-differe.description:
the Partzuf that originated from Keter was
named Arich Anpin, the Partzuf that originated from Hochma was
named Abba, and the Partzuf that originated from Bina was named
Ima.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-the-rule.description:
“Without a
screen, there is no male light, only female light, meaning light of
Nefesh.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

rabbi-yudah-said.description:
‘Great is the power
of prophets who resemble a form with its Maker, as it is written: ‘And

I heard the voice of a man between the banks of Ulai etc.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

two-kinds-of-reception-in-the-vessels.concept: Two kinds of reception in the vessels
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-ari\ndivides-reality-into-two-discer.concept:
The ARI
divides reality into two discernments
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

two-kinds-of-reception-in-the-vessel.concept: Two kinds of reception in the vessel
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-a-simple-reason-for-it.concept: There is a simple reason for it
there-is-a-simple-reason-for-it.description:
restriction means detainment
from wanting to receive.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-should-therefore-ask.description:
“How can light be
received without vessels of reception? After all, these three phases do
not contain any form of reception in them.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

now-you-can-understand-what-we-asked-abo.concept: Now you can understand what we asked above
now-you-can-understand-what-we-asked-abo.description:
“How can the upper
light expand only in three phases, while there are no vessels of
reception in these phases?” From the preceding explanation you can
see that now, the reception of that light also stems only from phase
four.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

three-fundamental-discernments-in-the-ve.concept: Three fundamental discernments in the vessels
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefirot-called.description:
Hochma, Bina, Zeir Anpin (which
consists of six Sefirot HGT NHY), and Malchut, and their root, called
Keter, which need an elaborate explanation.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

three-discernments.description:
the substance of the vessel: the force of
restriction in the vessel; the screen in it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

two-discernments-in-each-phase.description:
what it has from its superior;
what it has from its own essence.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-said.description:
Pleasure that comes to a person against his will, Abaie [name of a
sage] said – permitted; Raba [name of another sage] said –
“forbidden,” “possible and intending,” “impossible” and “intending,”
the whole world does not dispute that it is forbidden.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

abaie-and-raba-dispute.description:
Abaie thinks
that although it is possible, meaning that there is a choice to draw far
and refrain from enjoying the forbidden, it is still permitted to draw
near and enjoy it because he is not intending.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

in-the-first-three-degrees.description:
impossible and not intending, possible and
not intending, and impossible and intending, the prohibition on
reception is not agreed by all; only in phase four.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

malchut-of-keter-received-a-new-name.concept: Malchut of Keter received a new name
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

so-it-is\nwith-the-first-substance.concept:
So it is
with the first substance
so-it-is\nwith-the-first-substance.description:
it does not bear the name Hochma before it
receives its light, but is still included in Malchut of Keter.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-might-ask.description:
“But Malchut of Keter, when she became phase one in
the Sefira of Hochma, also had the same possibility not to take on that
new form of the will to receive.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-issue-of-the\nscreen.description:
because any extension of light is carried out in phase
four, there needs to be a detaining force that will prevent the
light from expanding into phase four.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-seems-to-be-a-question-here.concept: There seems to be a question here
there-seems-to-be-a-question-here.description:
Since the restriction occurred
primarily in phase four, it became evident that that phase is unworthy
of receiving the light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

malchut.description:
The first is the restricted Malchut, being the
first form that was made in Malchut of Ein Sof, which now adopted
the new form, called the “restricted Malchut.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-the-order.description:
First, the emanated being emerges with ten vessels –
Keter, Hochma, Bina, ZA (that consists of HGT NHY) and Malchut.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-a-simple-matter.description:
they do not have any coarseness because all their
four phases are equal (see Part 1, Chap 1, item 100).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

thus-there-are-now\nthree-lights-in-kete.concept:
Thus there are now
three lights in Keter
thus-there-are-now\nthree-lights-in-kete.description:
the light of Keter, the light of Hochma and the
light of Bina.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

hochma.description:
the light of
Hochma and the light of Bina, and one light in Bina, namely her own
light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

likewise-gevura-has-five-sefirot-of-dire.concept: Likewise Gevura has five Sefirot of direct light
likewise-gevura-has-five-sefirot-of-dire.description:
Gevura, Tifferet,
Netzah, Hod and Yesod.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

you-might-ask.description:
In the ten inner Sefirot of the above-mentioned general
Keter, there is no reflected light that rose from the general Malchut,
but only Nefesh, meaning the last part of the reflected light, the
smallest of the entire ten Sefirot of reflected light (see item 112), so
how can we say that that small part of reflected light has now
expanded by itself into ten new Sefirot of reflected light, to the point
of completing in each inner Sefira, all the inner of inner Sefirot that
they need in order to com
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-gave-hochma-the-two-lights-that-she-i.concept:
It gave Hochma the two lights that she is
missing
it-gave-hochma-the-two-lights-that-she-i.description:
one light for her own clothing, and another light in passing,
to clothe the Keter.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-two-conjectures-here.description:
One conjecture is that the earth has a
great force that pulls everything that is placed in the air, if there is
nothing solid to protect it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

that-name-has-two\nmeanings.description:
1) Distant illumination, 2) Certain illumination, meaning an
illumination that is certain to finally clothe there, since the light
“surrounds” it from all sides without giving her any room to escape
from it until she is fit to receive it in full.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefira.description:
lights of their own quality, and the lights that remained in them “in
passing.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

so-it-is-here.description:
In vessels that have no coarseness, such as the vessels of
the circles, it is considered that the light that comes there by the power
of the Sefirot of straightness becomes round because there is no

coarseness there, meaning yearning that would draw it with a pulling
force.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-how-the-degrees-cascade-from-one.concept: This is how the degrees cascade from one another
this-is-how-the-degrees-cascade-from-one.description:
Each lower degree is inferior to the higher one until the appearance of
this world, the most corrupted of them all.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefirot-of-its-own.description:
Hesed, Gevura, Tifferet,
Netzah, Hod, Yesod.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

malchut-would-be-regarded-as-two-sefirot.concept: Malchut would be regarded as two Sefirot
malchut-would-be-regarded-as-two-sefirot.description:
Malchut of direct
light and Malchut of reflected light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-revealed-only-under-two-conditio.concept: This is revealed only under two conditions
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

partzuf.description:
The bestowal measured in the additional coarseness is
regarded as internal and external, and reception and clothing in the
vessels are regarded as upper one and lower one.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

now-think.description:
Two Sefirot of her self, six of [direct light], and two of
reflected light are ten.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

second-cause.description: The first three phases are caused by one another.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

third-cause.description:
Malchut of Ein Sof is the cause for the Keter of the world
of restriction, because that craving to yearn for the greatest
equivalence of form that Malchut of Ein Sof acquired is regarded as
disparity of form in Malchut of Ein Sof.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

fourth-cause.description:
This Keter is the cause for the first restriction because it
expanded once more down to phase four in it (See answer 38), and
then restricted that will to receive and the light departed.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

fifth-cause.description:
The departure of the light that is done after the restriction
is the cause of the appearance of the vessels of the ten Sefirot of
circles (See answer 72).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sixth-cause.description:
The vessel of Malchut of circles, meaning phase four in
them, is the cause for the drawing of the upper light once more from
Ein Sof (See answers 83 and 138).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

seventh-cause.description:
The upper light that was drawn once more is the cause
of the appearance of the force of the screen in the vessel of Malchut
(See answer 43).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

eighth-cause.description:
The screen is the cause of the ten Sefirot of reflected
light that rise from it and above to the Keter of direct light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

ninth-cause.description:
The reflected light that rises from the screen is the cause
of the appearance of the vessels of straightness.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

tenth-cause.description:
The above-mentioned phase four, which received the
force of expansion through the reflected light, is the cause for the ten
Sefirot of the vessels of Adam Kadmon, which are called the Guf of
Adam Kadmon down to his Malchut (See answer 11).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

four-phases-in-the\ncoarseness-of-the-up.concept:
Four phases in the
coarseness of the upper light
four-phases-in-the\ncoarseness-of-the-up.description:
a) In its expansion from Keter to Hochma; b)
In its expansion to Bina, where distancing is added to it; c) In its expansion
to ZA, where a window and a narrow foramen were made in it; d) In its
expansion to Malchut, where a narrow foramen and a distancing were made
in it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

these-four-phases-are-called.description:
Hochma, Bina, ZA and Malchut and must be
present in every emanated being.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

i-said.description:
‘I will get wisdom’; but it
was far from me.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-the\nsame-with-our-matter.concept:
It is the
same with our matter
it-is-the\nsame-with-our-matter.description:
the screen on Malchut of Atzilut caused that reflected
light to rise and clothe all nine Sefirot of Atzilut and become vessels for
them.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-two-things-we-should-understan.concept: There are two things we should understand here
there-are-two-things-we-should-understan.description:
the first discernment
caused the reflected light that rises by the striking of the upper light in the
screen.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-ari-states-about-the-first-discernme.concept: The ARI states about the first discernment
the-ari-states-about-the-first-discernme.description:
“because of the striking of the
upper light in the curtain.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-ari-states-about\nthe-second-discern.concept:
The ARI states about
the second discernment
the-ari-states-about\nthe-second-discern.description: “by the power of the ten Sefirot of Atzilut” etc.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-are-five-discernments.description:
the coarsest screen, being phase four, reflects
the greatest measure of the reflected light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-the-same-in-the-world-of-assiya.concept: It is the same in the world of Assiya
it-is-the-same-in-the-world-of-assiya.description:
it
clothes the screen at the level of Malchut, and all ten Sefirot are in the light
of Malchut, even KHB ZA there.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

if-you-say.description:
It follows that ZA and Nukva of Atzilut and Beria are all
equal because they all receive from Bina of Atzilut, we should say that we
have already explained that Zeir and Nukva receive the light of Hochma
itself, for “you have made them all in wisdom,” and Bina is used only as a
passageway.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

consequential-rule.description:
The main difference between each two worlds in
ABYA is according to the screens in the four phases of the coarseness,
HB TM.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-find-that-there-is-a-consequential-ru.concept: We find that there is a consequential rule
we-find-that-there-is-a-consequential-ru.description:
Atzilut receives light only
from Hochma.

OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

these-are-the-four-phases-in-the\nlower-.concept:
These are the four phases in the
lower Adam
these-are-the-four-phases-in-the\nlower-.description:
a) the spirituality (Ruach) in him; b) the body; c) the
garments; d) the house one dwells in.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

adam.description:
NRNH,
body, garments, and the house one dwells in.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-are-the-four-worlds.description:
Atzilut, Beria, Yetzira, Assiya, which contain the
entire reality.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-median-phase-consists-of-two-sparks.concept: The median phase consists of two sparks
the-median-phase-consists-of-two-sparks.description:
a spark of the Creator, which
clothes the spark of the created being, in which there are the roots of
NRNH in man.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

that-is-why-the-ari-wrote.concept: That is why the ARI wrote
that-is-why-the-ari-wrote.description:
“That spark
clothes by the power of another spark, a created being.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

explanation.description:
It is necessary to have a median degree between the
Emanator and the emanated being, for the distance between them is as
between heaven and earth.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

keter-contains-two-phases.description:
the last phase of Ein Sof, which emanated a
second phase, which is the root of the emanated beings.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

We
(2)
have explained that each of these three worlds has one screen.

The word Ein implies absence of
attainment, while the word Ani refers to the absolute and total attainment.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-divided-into\nfour.description:
the root of Neshama is Hochma in Keter; Bina in Keter is the root to
the body; ZA in Keter is a root to the garment and Malchut in Keter is a root
to the hall.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-are-names-for-the-ten-sefirot-of-th.description:
Rosh is the Sefira of
Keter in it; the arms are HG: the Sefira of Hesed is the right arm, and the
Sefira of Gevura is the left arm.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

let-us-return-to-the-issue-of-the-guf.concept: Let us return to the issue of the Guf
let-us-return-to-the-issue-of-the-guf.description:
This phase is ten Sefirot, ten
(3)
measures, because they contain a boundary and a measure, as it is
written in Pirkey Heichalot, of the degree of 236 thousands of tens of
thousands parasangs [a measurement unit] etc.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-creator-was-clothed-with-ten-garment.concept: The Creator was clothed with ten garments
the-creator-was-clothed-with-ten-garment.description:
a garment of pride, as it
says, ‘the Lord reigns; He is clothed in majesty.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-yet-another-most-original\nreas.concept:
There is yet another most original
reason
there-is-yet-another-most-original\nreas.description:
the entire upper light that comes to Beria comes because of that
reflected light that rises by the power of the coupling by striking on the
screen of phase two in the bottom from her and above in Atzilut itself.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-imprinted-a-seal-of-atzilut\nin-ber.concept:
They imprinted a seal of Atzilut
in Beria
they-imprinted-a-seal-of-atzilut\nin-ber.description:
soul from soul, body from body, garment from garment and
hall from hall.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the tip of the Yod is the root,
the Yod is the soul, and the first Hey is the body, the Vav is the garment and
the last Hey is the hall.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-shall-first-examine-the-sefira-of-ket.concept: We shall first examine the Sefira of Keter
we-shall-first-examine-the-sefira-of-ket.description:
We find five phases of Keter here
in the words of the ARI.

It is so because they
all have one reason

the screen of phase four operates only in them and not
in any other world.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-five-phases-of-atzilut-are-souls.concept: The five phases of Atzilut are souls
the-five-phases-of-atzilut-are-souls.description:
for the roots, for the souls, for the
bodies, for the garments or for the halls.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-five-phases-of-yetzira-are\ngarments.concept:
The five phases of Yetzira are
garments
the-five-phases-of-yetzira-are\ngarments.description:
for the roots, for the souls, for the bodies, for the garments
or for halls.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-five-phases-of-assiya-are-halls.concept: The five phases of Assiya are halls
the-five-phases-of-assiya-are-halls.description:
for the roots, for the
souls, for the bodies, for the garments or for the halls.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

now-you-can-understand-the-words-of-our-.concept: Now you can understand the words of our sages

“His wife is like his
own body.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

another-way.description:
the lights in AK, meaning the souls in it, are called
Yechida.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-similar-in-the-garments.concept: It is similar in the garments
it-is-similar-in-the-garments.description:
the garments of AK are Yechida of

garments of ABYA, and the garments of Atzilut are Haya of garments
of AK and BYA etc.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

those-are.description: root, soul, body, garment and hall.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

these-are.description:
ten Sefirot of the root, ten Sefirot
of the soul, ten Sefirot of the body, ten Sefirot of the garment, and ten
Sefirot of the hall.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

heb.description:
dots/points],
after the ascent of Malchut, which is called a dot.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-surrounding-light-is-the-opposite.concept: The surrounding light is the opposite
the-surrounding-light-is-the-opposite.description:
it is known that the more
external light is greater .
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

assiya.description:
Yod in Atzilut is the name AB of Yodim, for “you have made
them all with wisdom [Hochma]” .

OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

with-a-filling-of-yodin-and\naleph.concept:
With a filling of Yodin and
Aleph

sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-ari-wrote-here.description: “As the value of the organ, so is its fineness.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-completely-similar-to-a-person.concept: It is completely similar to a person
it-is-completely-similar-to-a-person.description:
It is
inconceivable that only a small part of a single organ among all his organs
feels pain or pleasure, without the entire body, head to toe, feeling it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

know-that-there-is-yet-another-differenc.concept: Know that there is yet another difference
know-that-there-is-yet-another-differenc.description:
direct light was almost
separated from its place in order to descend and bestow upon the
(2)
lower ones.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

“Why was the expansion of the ten Sefirot of reflected light from
Malchut downward, to produce the ten Sefirot of the Guf, not enough, and a
second look was needed?”
Only one level of ten Sefirot emerged in the first look.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

we-could-ask.description:
“Therefore, how does the direct light pass from the Peh
downward, since the Peh, too, is the phase of Malchut, who does not
receive direct light from the restriction onward?” You already know that the
restriction was done on phase four in the sense of her being a vessel of
reception, meaning after Malchut had already expanded by herself, and
clothed the entire upper light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-first-three-phases.description:
Hochma, Bina, and Tifferet in Malchut, are suitable
for receiving direct light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

rosh-as.description:
Galgalta, Eynaim, Ozen, Hotem, Peh, and
the five phases of the Guf as: Keter, Hochma, Bina, Zeir Anpin, and
Malchut.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

nukva-as.description:
Galgalta, AB, SAG, MA, BON, in accordance with the Gematria
of the four fillings of the name HaVaYaH.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

for-instance.description:
Through the ascent of the Tabur of Partzuf of Galgalta to the
Peh of the Rosh of Galgalta, it receives coarseness of phase three there, on
whose coupling emerges the subsequent Partzuf, called AB.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

aramaic.description:
tendons), Hochma is called Atzamot (bones), and Keter is called Mochin
(marrow).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

for-example.description:
Behina Bet is a consequence, cause
by Behina Aleph and Behina Gimel by Behina Bet.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

because-they-are-two-complete-opposites.concept: Because they are two complete opposites
because-they-are-two-complete-opposites.description:
one wants only to bestow and to
not receive at all, and the other wants only to receive.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

behina-in-the\ntachton-has-two-roots.concept:
Behina in the
Tachton has two roots
behina-in-the\ntachton-has-two-roots.description:
one Shoresh in its corresponding Behina in the

Elyon, and a second Shoresh in Malchut, which is the general Shoresh of
the Tachton.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

sefirot-of-atzilut-are-two\nmatters.concept:
Sefirot of Atzilut are two
matters
sefirot-of-atzilut-are-two\nmatters.description:
The first is the expansion of spirituality, and the second is
(3)
the vessels and organs, where the self expands.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

partzufim.description:
AA,
AVI, and ZON in which the NRNHY clothe.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-lights-are-the-opposite.description:
in them
the lower ones appear first, beginning with the appearance of Nefesh, then

Ruach and finally Yechida.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

our-sages-wrote.description:
“In the beginning it came upon the thought to create the
world in the quality of judgment; He saw that the world does not exist,
preceded the quality of mercy and associated it with the quality of
judgment” (Beresheet Rabbah).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

they-ask.description:
“Since the first world of
Hesed had been created in a single utterance, meaning with a single vessel,
the vessel of Malchut, why then did ten Sefirot cascade and emerge from
it?” They answered: “To avenge the wicked, etc.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

ari.description:
the world of Akudim means that ten lights were
bound and tied in a single vessel.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-written-in-the-zohar.concept: It is written in The Zohar
it-is-written-in-the-zohar.description:
“Ein Sof
does not bring down His unification upon her until He is given his mate,”
which is the correction of phase four.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-four-phases-in-the-upper-light.concept: There are four phases in the upper light itself
there-are-four-phases-in-the-upper-light.description:
HB TM, even
before it expands for coupling by striking.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

so-is-the-manner\nof-the-upper-light.concept:
So is the manner
of the upper light
so-is-the-manner\nof-the-upper-light.description:
If it expands into some place, even when it departs from
there, it leaves there its entire form, not a single impression missing.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

c-t\nhapter-hree\nexplains-four-kinds-of.concept:
C T
HAPTER HREE
Explains four kinds of light
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

three-phases-of-lights.description:
a) the first expansion of Akudim from the Peh to the
Tabur; b) the records that remained from this the first expansion, which is
mercy; c) the levels of the reflected light that are extended from the
emanator during the departure of the lights, which is judgment.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-a-great-rule-in-the-wisdom.concept: This is a great rule in the wisdom
this-is-a-great-rule-in-the-wisdom.description:
The upper light flows and pours
incessantly, without any changes whatsoever, as it is written, “I the Lord do
not change,” as we have written in length (Inner Light, Chapter 2, item 2).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-ari-makes-this-precision.description:
“to the lower ones by them and through
them.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-yet-another-discernment.description:
Tifferet receives her light from the
posterior of the Sefira of Gevura.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-is-what\nthey-are-about.concept:
This is what
they are about
this-is-what\nthey-are-about.description:
The first light among them is called tastes, and the
third light, called reflected light, is called dots, since dots are discerned
as judgment.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-a-great-thing-that-he-tells-us-her.concept: It is a great thing that he tells us here
it-is-a-great-thing-that-he-tells-us-her.description:
it is known that the 288 sparks
that remained in the vessels after they were broken and died were the cause
of the revival of the vessels.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

three-discernments-in-the-ten-sefirot.concept: Three discernments in the ten Sefirot
three-discernments-in-the-ten-sefirot.description:
Keter, the other
Sefirot, and Malchut.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-another-reason.description:
when her light rose, it did not leave any
record in her at all.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-is-a-second-reason.description:
if the
externality of the vessel had not been refined, the surrounding light would
not have been able to connect with the inner light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-second-question\nis.description:
if the internality of the vessel is indeed far more important than the
externality of the vessel, it should have extended from a more important
root, from phase two of the Rosh called Ozen, and the externality of the
vessel from a lower root, phase three.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-answer-is-this.description: know that the light is in complete evenness .
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

the-answer-is-as-follows.description:
Although the inner light is
(50)
smaller than the surrounding light, nevertheless, because it is confined
inside the vessel , the vessel receives complete illumination from it.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

he-asks.description:
In the end, we find the externality of the vessels to be less
important than the internality of the vessels, and they do not shine like they
do.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

here-there-are-three-things-we-must-know.concept: Here there are three things we must know first
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

ruach-has\ntwo-vessels.description:
the root in the Keter is Ruach of Ruach, and in Hochma it is
Nefesh of Ruach.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

zeir-anpin-is-in-the-following-manner.concept: Zeir Anpin is in the following manner
zeir-anpin-is-in-the-following-manner.description:
when Yesod
emerged, the general five edges of Zeir Anpin appeared only in the
phase of Nefesh.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

malchut-is-the-opposite.description:
she left
first and entered last .
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

second-version.description:
It follows from the manuscript of Baal
HaSulam that this commentary is redundant since there are no more than
five inner and two surrounding, as the ARI said here in item 19].
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-two-kinds-of-reflected-light.concept: There are two kinds of reflected light
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

it-is-like-a-person-who-walks-out-of-the.concept: It is like a person who walks out of the house
it-is-like-a-person-who-walks-out-of-the.description:
even though he takes, for
example, four steps on his way out of the house, we still don’t regard them
as being four rests in his walk; this is completely unthinkable.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

so-is-the-issue-before-us.concept: So is the issue before us
so-is-the-issue-before-us.description:
Even though the screen passes four phases during
the departure, and the upper light makes a coupling with it on its way, it is
still not regarded as expansion of the upper light into the Partzuf, as mercy.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

there-are-two-flaws-in-this-reflected-li.concept: There are two flaws in this reflected light
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

this-reflected-light-has-two-flaws.concept: This reflected light has two flaws
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

explanation-of-the-words.description:
Everything that is found in the light is also
found in the record that remains of that light.
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

here-the-ari-sets-a-great-rule-for-us.concept: Here the ARI sets a great rule for us
here-the-ari-sets-a-great-rule-for-us.description:
“The expansion of the light and
its latter departure, caused the making of the vessel” (see here, Chapter

1, item 9).
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag

hod.description: A concept from Kabbalah mentioned in  OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
sources: OceanofPDF.com Talmud Eser Sefirot Vol 1 - Rav Yehuda Leib Ashlag
